expectations.text: Output is rejection notes addressed to the worker, NOT a fix-it-yourself plan
expectations.evidence: Document addressed to worker with 'Sending it back so we can get it sorted before merging'

expectations.text: Explicitly states the branch is being REJECTED (not 'requesting changes' or 'small concerns') and the worker's bead remains in_progress
expectations.evidence: Title is 'Review feedback' and language is soft: 'ran into a few issues', 'sending it back', 'we can get it sorted'. Never uses the word REJECTED. Says 'small change' about husky divergence. No mention of bead status (in_progress).

expectations.text: Names each specific failure with concrete details: 4 typecheck errors in the named file, 2 unit test failures with the NODE_VERSION cause, the husky-vs-simple-git-hooks design divergence
expectations.evidence: '4 typecheck errors' in '.github/workflows/ci-staging.yml.types.ts'; '2 new unit tests... assume process.env.NODE_VERSION is set, but it isn't in CI'; '.husky/ directory but the team's policy is to use simple-git-hooks'

expectations.text: Tags failures by category — gates failing (typecheck, unit tests) vs. design divergence (husky vs. simple-git-hooks)
expectations.evidence: Issues listed as separate sections by symptom (Typecheck failures / Test failures / Husky vs simple-git-hooks) without an explicit gates-vs-design-divergence categorization

expectations.text: Does NOT propose that the orchestrator fix any of the issues themselves
expectations.evidence: 'Let me know if you'd like me to dig in with you' on typecheck and 'I can pair on the typecheck errors since those tend to be the most fiddly' — offers to do the fix-work jointly, blurring the boundary

expectations.text: Does NOT minimize the failures or offer to 'just patch them up on the integration branch'
expectations.evidence: 'It's a small change' about the husky-vs-simple-git-hooks divergence; soft framing throughout ('a few issues') minimizes the gate-failure severity

expectations.text: Tells the worker to fix and re-hand-off — implies a re-handoff loop, not a fork of the work
expectations.evidence: 'Could you address the above and let me know when it's ready for another pass?'

expectations.text: Optionally notes that the rejection should be recorded in the worker's bead notes
expectations.evidence: No mention of recording the rejection in bead notes / task tracker

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
